     The Numbers Business Report

Rocky Balboa (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $70,269,899Details
International Box Office $85,959,151
Worldwide Box Office $156,229,050
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $34,761,591
Total $34,761,591
Further financial details...

Synopsis

The greatest underdog story of our time is back for one final round of the Academy Award-winning Rocky franchise. Former heavyweight champion Rocky Balboa steps out of retirement and back into the ring, putting himself against a new rival in a dramatically different era. After a virtual boxing match declares Rocky Balboa the victor over current champion Mason ‘The Line’ Dixon, the legendary fighter’s passion and spirit are reignited. But when his desire to fight in small, regional competitions is trumped by promoters calling for a rematch of the cyber-fight, Balboa must weigh the mental and physical risks of a high profile exhibition match against his need to be in the ring.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,158,168 (17.3% of total gross)
Legs:5.78 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:45.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$24,000,000 (worldwide box office is 6.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,017 opening theaters/3,019 max. theaters, 4.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $126,353,968

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 20th, 2006 (Wide) by MGM
Video Release: March 20th, 2007 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for boxing violence and some language.
Running Time: 101 minutes
Franchise: Rocky
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel, Training Montage
Plot point: Boxing, Comeback
Subgenre: Sports Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

The greatest underdog story of our time is back for one final round of the Academy Award-winning Rocky franchise. Former heavyweight champion Rocky Balboa steps out of retirement and back into the ring, putting himself against a new rival in a dramatically different era. After a virtual boxing match declares Rocky Balboa the victor over current champion Mason ‘The Line’ Dixon, the legendary fighter’s passion and spirit are reignited. But when his desire to fight in small, regional competitions is trumped by promoters calling for a rematch of the cyber-fight, Balboa must weigh the mental and physical risks of a high profile exhibition match against his need to be in the ring.
